prompt_text: Hightouch vs Omnata pricing and features
response:
Hightouch and Omnata are both data activation tools, but they price very differently: **Hightouch** uses a destination-based model with a free tier and paid plans that are often quote-based, while **Omnata** starts with a self-serve plan at **$20 per user/month** and says it does **not charge for data volume or compute** because it runs natively inside Snowflake.[3][6][7]

| Category | Hightouch | Omnata |
|---|---|---|
| **Pricing model** | Destination-based; free tier plus paid plans, with many enterprise deals quote-based[1][3][4] | Per-user self-serve pricing starting at **$20/user/month**; higher-volume customers contact sales[7] |
| **Free tier / trial** | Free tier available; paid plans have a 30-day free trial[1][3] | Self-serve tier mentioned; no free tier is clearly stated in the results[7] |
| **Cost drivers** | Number of billable destinations, plus plan level and features[1][4] | User count; no data-volume or compute charges per Omnata’s native architecture claim[6][7] |
| **Core focus** | Reverse ETL / composable CDP for syncing warehouse data to many destinations[1][3][4] | Native Snowflake-based integration, especially for Snowflake ↔ Salesforce-style workflows[6] |
| **Connectors / destinations** | About **150+** destinations in one result, and **250+** in another, depending on source date and product scope[1][3][4] | The provided results emphasize Snowflake-native integration rather than broad connector breadth[6][7] |
| **Scheduling / activation** | Sync scheduling, including hourly or more frequent syncs on higher plans[1] | Low-latency native syncs, with one source claiming **as low as 1 minute**[6] |
| **Security / compliance** | SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA mentioned in results[1][3] | Native-in-Snowflake architecture is highlighted as simplifying security approval[6] |

A practical takeaway is that **Hightouch** is usually the better fit if you want broader reverse-ETL destination coverage and a mature activation platform, while **Omnata** may be cheaper and simpler if your workflow is centered in **Snowflake** and you want to avoid separate data-handling infrastructure.[1][3][4][6]

There is one caveat: the search results do not fully agree on Hightouch pricing details. One source says Hightouch has a free tier plus plans named **Free, Starter, Pro, and Business**[1], while another says the only clearly self-serve entry point is a free tier and that most pricing is custom quote-based[3].
